猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'neutral aromatic' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap is an explicit pairwise closure rationale separating neutral aromatic from ICT on this molecule using evidence more discriminative than generic mixed close-family context.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is neutral aromatic and top2 is TICT after reweighting, with unknown still slightly above ICT because ICT continues to accumulate repeated weakening or missing-evidence signals without any direct positive support family. I interpret the latest verifier evidence as high-information but mixed close-family context: it reinforces that related systems can show neutral-aromatic/AIE/RIR or locally excited behavior, while also keeping donor-acceptor/TICT/ICT language present in the surrounding family literature. This verifier evidence does not reduce portfolio coverage debt because coverage debt was already closed; instead it supports pairwise contraction contextually, but not decisively. No still-credible hypothesis remains untested as active portfolio debt: TICT is explicitly blocked by capability, ICT is dropped with reason, ESIPT is directly weakened, and unknown is residual only. The case therefore remains legitimately in pairwise contraction rather than portfolio screening because the current top1 has direct support families in the ledger. Closure justification is still collecting rather than sufficient, because the verifier retrieval remained close-family and mixed and did not cleanly close neutral aromatic versus ICT on this exact molecule. The case is not ready for decisive finalize or best-available finalize yet; one more bounded verifier supplement targeted to neutral aromatic versus ICT is warranted.

Best-available closure keeps 'neutral aromatic' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap is an explicit pairwise closure rationale separating neutral aromatic from ICT on this molecule using evidence more discriminative than generic mixed close-family context.

The latest verifier retrieval was high-information but effectively still mixed and not cleanly aligned to the remaining closure pair. Although it contains ICT-relevant topics, the completed verifier evidence record is still better characterized as partial support for neutral aromatic versus ICT closure rather than sufficient decisive supplementation.

Closure is still collecting because the remaining decisive need is a sharper pairwise rationale for neutral aromatic versus ICT on this exact molecule, and current evidence is still mixed close-family support rather than decisive exact-identity discrimination.

Portfolio screening remains complete. The latest verifier evidence adds pairwise context and does not reopen any unresolved hypothesis screen. TICT remains blocked rather than untested, while ICT, ESIPT, and unknown remain outside coverage debt with explicit reasons.

Macro capabilities have been reliable and useful for molecule-specific structural screening, especially for neutral-aromatic support. Verifier capability is currently the best active route for bounded pairwise discrimination. Microscopic Amesp-dependent routes are stalled by repeated runtime failure and should not be rescheduled on the same observable without a route change. Recent working memory repeats the same verifier local uncertainty, so repeating the same verifier route is unlikely to shrink the current gap: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

The latest verifier retrieved 4 close-family evidence cards covering AIE, RIR, neutral_aromatic, locally_excited_state, donor-acceptor, solvatochromism, dual_fluorescence, hydrazone, ICT, TICT, C=N rotation, phenol, and retrieval limitation topics. No exact-identity hit was found. This adds high-information but mixed external context and does not override the existing molecule-specific neutral-aromatic structural support or the repeated ESIPT-preorganization weakening.
